name: Penciljam Bengaluru at MAP
description:
We are drawing at the Museum of Art and Photography this Sunday.

The Museum of Art & Photography (MAP), located in the heart of Bengaluru, is dedicated to making art accessible and engaging for all. Visit their website to see the current exhibits.

We are delighted to continue to partner with MAP who has made the space available for this session from 10:30am to 1pm. A quite day to immerse ourselves at the Museum.
How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.

Please register to attend this event. (Registration link will be updated here)

Please arrive at the museum before 10:30 am to join the session brief. If you are familiar with these jams, once you arrive at the location, choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable.
Once you arrive at MAP

 	Inform the front desk that you are there for Penciljam,
 	Confirm your name against the RSVP list, you can also register on the spot for this event
 	Leave your bag in the lockers at the basement level.
 	You can carry an A4/A5 or smaller sketchbook and portable dry material kit to draw within.
 	Choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable - be mindful of not blocking the path of visitors to the museum.

Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper (A4/A5 or smaller sketchbook)
 	Art materials of your choice - Pencils or any dry medium. (within the museum we cannot use wet media like paints. Only non-messy dry media is allowed)

How to get there
This building is opposite the Government Museum.
Follow the location map provided
Closest metro station: Cubbon Park Metro station (900m from MAP)

name: Penciljam Bengaluru at Cubbon Park | High Court Gate
description:
The Karnataka High Court, housed in the iconic Attara Kacheri building, is one of Bengaluru’s most striking landmarks. Built in 1868 in Greco-Roman styled architecture, its vibrant red façade and grand columns stand out against the green expanse of Cubbon Park.

We are drawing at the back gate, where we have a view of the high court, the band stand, cubbon statue all in line to the Government Museum. Remember to arrive at the location through Cubbon Park.
How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.

If this is your first time and would like future update on events, and more. Please register here. Once you arrive at the location, choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable.
Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper/drawing surface.
 	Any medium you would like to use/learn/try.
 	A portable chair/mat. If the location has no seating, we tend to sit on the ground.
 	Water bottle and snacks.

How to get there
Follow the location map provided
Closest metro station: MG road

name: Penciljam Bengaluru at Nrityagram
description:
Founded in 1990 by visionary Odissi dancer Protima Gauri, **Nrityagram** stands as India’s first modern **dance village**—a Gurukula-inspired residential community where artists live, train, and create within an immersive, purpose-built campus. T he Nrityagram Dance Ensemble, advances classical traditions through rigorous training, creative innovation, and acclaimed global performances.

This rare confluence of art, architecture, and community makes Nrityagram not only a sanctuary for the spirit of Indian classical dance but also a living testament to its continuing evolution.



How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.

If this is your first time and would like future update on events, and more. Please register here. Once you arrive at the location, choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable.

There is an entry ticket at Nrityagram, INR 150 per person.



Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper/drawing surface.
 	Any medium you would like to use/learn/try.
 	A portable chair/mat. If the location has no seating, we tend to sit on the ground.
 	Water bottle and snacks.
 	Carry some food as this space has no canteen ( Food cannot be eaten inside the premises)




Notes for this location 

 	There is an entry ticket of 150/- per person charged by Nrityagram.
 	We meet near the Nrityagram office for a quick brief at 11am. We reconvene at the same space at 1pm for the Sketch Showcase.
 	Please carry rain coats or umbrella just in case, although the campus itself has enough shelter in case of rain
 	Carry lunch / snacks as this space has no canteen, food can be consumed outside the campus.
 	Please do not litter. All litter must be carried back to the city to be disposed of.
 	Photography of dancers and dance classes is not allowed.
 	Participants are requested to maintain silence during the classes, and in the campus. If you wish to listen to music, please carry headphones.




How to get there
As suggested on the Nrityagram website 
Nrityagram is 35 kilometres from MG Road and 5 kilometres from Hessaraghatta village. There are several options to get here:

 	Private Transport: car or two wheeler
 	Bus

 	From City Market take bus number 266A, 253C, 253D, 253E
 	From Majestic take bus number 253J
 	Buses stop at Hessaraghatta village, where you can get an auto-rickshaw (Approximately ₹150/200 one-way).


 	Metro

 	The closest Metro station is Dasarahalli.
 	From Dasarahalli take bus number 253D, 253E, 253F or 253J to Hessaraghatta village, where you can get an auto-rickshaw (Approximately INR 150/200 one-way).


 	Don't forget to be in touch with the community via discord as you travel perhaps you can find travel companions to this location.




Group Transport (Optional)
Details will be up 10 days before the jam.
